function_explanation: Renders a single Gaussian particle onto an image by adding Gaussian-distributed intensity values centered at a specified position.
function_docstring:
Render a single Gaussian particle onto the image.

Adds a Gaussian intensity distribution to the image centered at the specified
coordinates (x0, y0). The Gaussian is computed within a bounded region around
the center point (3 sigma radius) to optimize performance.

Args:
    img: A 2D numpy array representing the image to render onto. The particle
        intensity values will be added in-place to this array. Shape is (H, W)
        where H is the image height and W is the image width.
    x0: The x-coordinate (column) of the particle center in pixel units.
        Can be a float for sub-pixel positioning.
    y0: The y-coordinate (row) of the particle center in pixel units.
        Can be a float for sub-pixel positioning.
    diameter: The diameter of the particle. Used to calculate the Gaussian
        standard deviation as sigma = diameter / 4.0.
    intensity: The peak intensity value of the Gaussian particle.
        Defaults to 255.0.

Returns:
    numpy.ndarray: The input image array with the Gaussian particle added.
        The modification is done in-place, so the returned array is the
        same object as the input img.
usage_example:
import numpy as np

# Create a blank image
img = np.zeros((100, 100), dtype=np.float64)

# Render a Gaussian particle at position (50, 50) with diameter 10
img = generate_gaussian_particle(img, x0=50.0, y0=50.0, diameter=10.0, intensity=255.0)

# Render multiple particles
img = generate_gaussian_particle(img, x0=30.0, y0=70.0, diameter=8.0, intensity=200.0)
img = generate_gaussian_particle(img, x0=75.5, y0=25.5, diameter=12.0, intensity=180.0)
